task_completion.description: Create an SVG file named 'star.svg' containing a star icon with a yellow fill and black stroke.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file manager, or text editor showing the creation or editing of 'star.svg'. The file content should show valid SVG tags (e.g., <polygon> or <path> forming a star) with attributes for yellow fill (fill="#FFFF00" or fill="yellow") and black stroke (stroke="black" or stroke="#000000").

task_completion.description: Create a new style named 'star_marker' in GeoServer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoServer 'Styles' page showing a new style being created or successfully saved with the exact name 'star_marker'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'star_marker' style to use 'star.svg' as an ExternalGraphic with a size between 16-24px.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SLD code editor in the GeoServer style page showing a <PointSymbolizer> containing an <ExternalGraphic> block. It must include an <OnlineResource> pointing to 'star.svg' (e.g., xlink:href="star.svg"), <Format>image/svg+xml</Format>, and a <Size> element with a value between 16 and 24.

task_completion.description: Assign 'star_marker' as the default style for the 'ne_populated_places' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoServer 'Edit Layer' page for the 'ne_populated_places' layer. Under the 'Publishing' tab, the 'Default Style' dropdown must have 'star_marker' selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the style is applied correctly to the layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoServer Layer Preview (OpenLayers map) for 'ne_populated_places' showing the map points rendered as yellow stars with black outlines.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GeoServer web administration interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoServer web UI (http://localhost:8080/geoserver/web/) being navigated, rather than just API calls via terminal.

integrity.description: Agent created a genuine SVG file rather than a placeholder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'star.svg' file contents shown in the text editor or terminal should contain actual vector path/polygon data for a star, not just empty or dummy XML tags.

integrity.description: The layer preview is genuinely generated by GeoServer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map preview should be inside the standard GeoServer OpenLayers preview interface with standard controls, showing the actual Natural Earth populated places dataset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target SVG filename: 'star.svg'. Target style name: 'star_marker'. Target layer: 'ne_populated_places'. The SVG must contain a yellow fill (#FFFF00 or 'yellow') and a black stroke. The GeoServer SLD configuration must use <ExternalGraphic> referencing 'star.svg' with a <Size> between 16 and 24. Look for these exact names and values in the GeoServer UI and text editor screenshots.
